summary: Mutation testing infrastructure failed (Stryker vitest runner unable to find test files via related-file detection). Manual analysis performed on 73 potential mutants across src/sdk/api-client.ts. Tests cover all primary code paths. Three untested branches identified: (1) successful non-JSON response return, (2) empty body in non-JSON error fallback, (3) custom header passthrough. Overall test quality is high with good boundary condition coverage.
testQuality: Tests are comprehensive for the primary request/response lifecycle. The refactor commit added meaningful boundary tests (non-JSON errors, fallback messages, auth header verification for both GET and POST, Content-Type presence/absence). Three minor gaps exist but do not represent significant risk.

suggestions.description: Successful non-JSON response path not tested
suggestions.rationale: When response.ok is true but body is not valid JSON, the function returns the raw text. No test verifies this path.
suggestions.suggestion: Add test: mock a 200 response with non-JSON text body and assert the returned value equals that text string

suggestions.description: Empty body in non-JSON error path uses fallback message
suggestions.rationale: The expression `text || Request failed` has two branches but only the non-empty text branch is tested via the 502 HTML test.
suggestions.suggestion: Add test: mock a 502 response with empty string body and assert error message is `502: Request failed`

suggestions.description: Custom headers in options.headers are not tested for passthrough
suggestions.rationale: The spread `...options?.headers` merges caller-provided headers but no test verifies that custom headers survive the merge.
suggestions.suggestion: Add test: pass custom headers in request options and assert they appear in the fetch call alongside Authorization
